LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WENDY PASKIN-JORDAN AND FRANCIS JORDAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPHS 5 AND 23 OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF J. CRISTINA GINER IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPHS 5 AND 23 ARE NOT NECESSARILY IRRELEVANT. THE ALLEGATIONS, FOR INSTANCE, MAY BE RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF DEFENDANTS' CONTROL AND/OR RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE SIDEWALK. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Set For Trial On May-20-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Francis Jordan, Wendy Paskin-Jordan'S Motion To Strike Part Of Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendants Wendy Paskin-Jordan and Francis Jordan's motion to strike paragraphs 5 and 23 of the complaint filed by plaintiff J. Cristina Giner is denied. The allegations in paragraphs 5 and 23 are not necessarily irrelevant. The allegations, for instance, may be relevant to the issue of defendants' control and/or responsibility for the sidewalk. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEFENDANT FRANCIS JORDAN, WENDY PASKIN-JORDAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PART OF COMPLAINT.

Continued to October 16, 2018, per agreement of all parties (email 9-26-18).

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
